title: system tests: start_registry: pasta: Failed to bind port 4xxxx (Address already in use)
body:
Weird one, seen only twice in 1 month:
```
[+0226s] not ok 133 [150] podman login - basic test in 2584ms
... running start_registry ....
<+2.13s> # $ podman --storage-driver vfs --root /tmp/CI_HpL4/bats-run-bGGd21/suite/podman-bats-registry/root --runroot /tmp/CI_HpL4/bats-run-bGGd21/suite/podman-bats-registry/runroot --tmpdir /tmp/CI_HpL4/bats-run-bGGd21/suite/podman-bats-registry/tmpdir run -d -p 127.0.0.1:42188:5000 --name registry -v /tmp/CI_HpL4/bats-run-bGGd21/suite/podman-bats-registry/auth:/auth:Z -e REGISTRY_AUTH=htpasswd -e REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_REALM=Registry Realm -e REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_PATH=/auth/htpasswd -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_CERTIFICATE=/auth/domain.crt -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_KEY=/auth/domain.key quay.io/libpod/registry:2.8.2
<+157ms> # Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:
         # Failed to bind port 42188 (Address already in use) for option '-t 127.0.0.1/42188-42188:5000-5000', exiting
```
Not a parallelization bug. I'm completely stumped.

* debian-13 : sys podman debian-13 rootless host sqlite
    * [08-19 09:33](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5315013513052160/html/sys-podman-debian-13-rootless-host-sqlite.log.html#t--00044) in [sys] setup_file failed
* fedora-40 : sys podman fedora-40 rootless host boltdb
  * PR #23275
    * [09-03 21:37](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4583306795155456/html/sys-podman-fedora-40-rootless-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00133) in [sys] [150] podman login - basic test

| x | x | x | x | x | x |
| ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| sys(2) | podman(2) | fedora-40(1) | rootless(2) | host(2) | sqlite(1) |
|  |  | debian-13(1) |  |  | boltdb(1) |

comments.body:
Ports in the range in /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_local_port_range `32768-60999` will be used as ephemeral range so any process binding to port 0 or connect somewhere else (outgoing connection) can get ports from this range assigned.
That races with our port is free logic as we check if it is free and then later use it, if another process got it between that we fail. That doesn't mean it is the cause here but I would recommend against using ports from this range to avoid such races.
Treating this one as closed by #24002
